perturbation.original_text: 2. Term. The Term shall commence and end on the dates set forth in the Agreement Summary, unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement, provided, however, that in no event shall the Term extend beyond the term of the Joint Use and Management Agreement by and between the City of San Diego and Padres, L.P. dated as of February 1, 2000.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Term. The Term shall commence and end on the dates set forth in the Agreement Summary, unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement. SDBF has the right to extend the term unilaterally, at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the term of the agreement. Originally, the term was fixed as defined in the Agreement Summary, subject to early termination or the Joint Use Agreement end. The addition of 'SDBF has the right to extend the term unilaterally, at their discretion' now conflicts directly with the initial, defined end date. Section 1 outlines that the term is defined in the agreement summary but this modification provides SDBF with the right to extend the term unilaterally, at their discretion. Contradicting with the initial agreed term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM: The term (the "Term") shall commence on November 1, 2007 and end on October 31, 2008 (unless terminated earlier in accordance with the General Provisions).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by granting SDBF the unilateral right to extend the term, directly conflicting with the 'TERM' section, which specifies a fixed end date of October 31, 2008. This contradicts the initial agreement on the term's duration, making the agreement's term uncertain and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Indemnity; Insurance; and Assumption of the Risk. a. SDBF agrees to indemnify, protect, defend, and hold harmless Sponsor, its affiliates, predecessors and successors, owners, agents, partners, officials, employees and representatives (collectively, the "Sponsor Parties") from and against any and all actions, demands, liabilities, losses, claims, damages, costs or expenses, including without limitation court costs and attorneys' fees (collectively, the "Claims"), arising from the negligence or willful misconduct of SDBF or any of its affiliates, predecessors and successors, owners, agents, partners, officials, employees or representatives. In the event that any Claim is brought against any of Sponsor Parties, then, upon receipt of notification of such Claim, SDBF will assume the defense of such Claim and, upon the request of one or more of Sponsor Parties, will permit such party or parties to participate in the defense, such participation to be at such party's expense. This provision shall survive any cancellation or termination of this Agreement as to activities which occurred while this Agreement was in force.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Indemnity; Insurance; and Assumption of the Risk. a. SDBF agrees to indemnify, protect, defend, and hold harmless Sponsor, its affiliates, predecessors and successors, owners, agents, partners, officials, employees and representatives (collectively, the "Sponsor Parties") from and against any and all actions, demands, liabilities, losses, claims, damages, costs or expenses, including without limitation court costs and attorneys' fees (collectively, the "Claims"), arising from the negligence or willful misconduct of SDBF or any of its affiliates, predecessors and successors, owners, agents, partners, officials, employees or representatives, except for claims arising from actions undertaken at the direction of the Sponsor. In the event that any Claim is brought against any of Sponsor Parties, then, upon receipt of notification of such Claim, SDBF will assume the defense of such Claim and, upon the request of one or more of Sponsor Parties, will permit such party or parties to participate in the defense, such participation to be at such party's expense. This provision shall survive any cancellation or termination of this Agreement as to activities which occurred while this Agreement was in force.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction in the indemnity clause. Initially, SDBF indemnifies Sponsor against claims arising from SDBF's negligence or willful misconduct. The added exception, 'except for claims arising from actions undertaken at the direction of the Sponsor,' now makes SDBF responsible for actions they did as a result of Sponsor's direction. This can lead to uncertainty about what actions fall under which party's responsibility and can create a dispute over what constitutes acting at the direction of the Sponsor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SDBF agrees to indemnify, protect, defend, and hold harmless Sponsor, its affiliates, predecessors and successors, owners, agents, partners, officials, employees and representatives (collectively, the "Sponsor Parties") from and against any and all actions, demands, liabilities, losses, claims, damages, costs or expenses, including without limitation court costs and attorneys' fees (collectively, the "Claims"), arising from the negligence or willful misconduct of SDBF or any of its affiliates, predecessors and successors, owners, agents, partners, officials, employees or representatives.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception to SDBF's indemnification obligation ('except for claims arising from actions undertaken at the direction of the Sponsor'). This directly contradicts the original indemnification clause, which states SDBF agrees to indemnify Sponsor for all claims arising from the negligence or willful misconduct of SDBF without such exception. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty in the contract regarding liability for actions taken under the Sponsor's direction, fulfilling the definition of a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction, as the responsibility is conflicted. The contradiction is that SDBF indemnifies for all claims due to their negligence but also doesn't indemnify for claims arising from actions undertaken at the direction of the sponsor, even if the action done at the direction of the sponsor was due to the negligence of SDBF.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Default. If (i) Sponsor fails to pay any amounts within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) Sponsor otherwise defaults in the performance or observance of Sponsor's duties and obligations under this Agreement and fails to cure such default within fifteen (15) days after notice thereof by SDBF, or (iii) Sponsor makes an assignment of substantially all of its assets for the benefit of creditors, is adjudicated bankrupt, files a voluntary petition in bankruptcy or a voluntary petition or an answer seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under Title 11 of the United States Code or any successor or other federal or state insolvency law ("Bankruptcy Law"), has filed against it an involuntary petition in bankruptcy or seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under any Bankruptcy Law, which petition is not discharged within thirty (30) days, or applies for or permits the appointment of a receiver or trustee for its assets, SDBF may, at its option, with or without notice or demand, (x) terminate the rights of Sponsor under this Agreement, whereupon SDBF shall have no further obligation of any kind to Sponsor, and Sponsor shall have no right to recovery or offset of any amounts previously paid to SDBF under this Agreement, and (y) exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF. Any notice required to be given hereunder shall be in lieu of, and not in addition to, any notice required under California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1161 or any similar or successor law.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Default. If (i) Sponsor fails to pay any amounts within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) Sponsor otherwise defaults in the performance or observance of Sponsor's duties and obligations under this Agreement and fails to cure such default within fifteen (15) days after notice thereof by SDBF, or (iii) Sponsor makes an assignment of substantially all of its assets for the benefit of creditors, is adjudicated bankrupt, files a voluntary petition in bankruptcy or a voluntary petition or an answer seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under Title 11 of the United States Code or any successor or other federal or state insolvency law ("Bankruptcy Law"), has filed against it an involuntary petition in bankruptcy or seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under any Bankruptcy Law, which petition is not discharged within thirty (30) days, or applies for or permits the appointment of a receiver or trustee for its assets, SDBF may, at its option, with or without notice or demand, (x) terminate the rights of Sponsor under this Agreement, whereupon SDBF shall have no further obligation of any kind to Sponsor, and Sponsor shall have no right to recovery or offset of any amounts previously paid to SDBF under this Agreement, and (y) exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF, unless the Sponsor provides a written guarantee from a Major League Baseball team guaranteeing payments. Any notice required to be given hereunder shall be in lieu of, and not in addition to, any notice required under California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1161 or any similar or successor law.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction to the default clause. The original clause gives SDBF the right to terminate the agreement if the Sponsor defaults on payments or obligations. Adding the exception 'unless the Sponsor provides a written guarantee from a Major League Baseball team guaranteeing payments' creates an uncertainty because previously SDBF has no further obligation of any kind to the Sponsor. The addition of a guarantee opens a loophole, contradicting the absolute nature of the termination and its consequences as previously stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SDBF may, at its option, with or without notice or demand, (x) terminate the rights of Sponsor under this Agreement, whereupon SDBF shall have no further obligation of any kind to Sponsor, and Sponsor shall have no right to recovery or offset of any amounts previously paid to SDBF under this Agreement, and (y) exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.justification: The added clause 'unless the Sponsor provides a written guarantee from a Major League Baseball team guaranteeing payments' directly contradicts the original clause stating 'SDBF shall have no further obligation of any kind to Sponsor' upon termination due to default. This creates a loophole, potentially obligating SDBF to continue the agreement or renegotiate if a guarantee is provided, thus undermining the absolute termination clause and introducing a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction as per the definitions provided.
